channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The iPhone 16E is here. Here are 5 ways it’s different from the iPhone SE
item.description: Apple has introduced its first new product of 2025: the iPhone 16E. The new iPhone replaces the iPhone SE from Apple’s lineup—the company’s entry-level, budget iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Instagram wants you to do more with DMs than just slide into someone else’s
item.description: Instagram is making some major changes to DMs, and they just might make you want to use them way more.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO
item.description: Katie Arrington's return to DoD comes as her lawyers work out a settlement with the government related to the suspension of her security clearance in 2021. The post Lead CMMC architect returns to Pentagon as DoD CISO first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Zuckerberg philanthropy ends DEI programs
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's philanthropy has shuttered its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs as well as work on immigration reform as it reduces social advocacy work, the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative (CZI) announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google Lens just got an upgrade on your iPhone. Here’s how it works
item.description: Google Lens is getting gesture support, making it easier and quicker to search in the Google and Chrome apps.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: New iPhone 16e renews Apple’s push into budget smartphone market
item.description: Apple has faced increased competition from Google and Samsung, in the budget smartphone market and in the AI space
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DeepSeek accused of sharing users' data with TikTok's ByteDance in another blow around privacy concerns
item.description: According to South Korea's data regulator, the two Chinese tech firms are "communicating." Here's what's at stake for users' privacy.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Aryaka adds AI-powered observability to SASE platform
item.description: SASE vendor Aryaka is looking to alleviate network alert fatigue with a little help from AI. Today Aryaka announced its Winter 2025 platform update, introducing its new AI Observe tool for advanced threat detection and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 ways to fix OneDrive’s worst annoyances on Windows
item.description: On Windows, OneDrive is built right into the operating system. Even if you don’t turn it on, it will pop up occasionally, asking you to “back up your files to the cloud.” What that really means, of course, is that Microsoft wants you to move your folders to OneDrive. I’m not here to knock OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Looking for Gemini on iOS? It’s now only in Google’s dedicated Gemini app – complete with the new research mode for subscribers
item.description: Gemini is no longer available in the Google app on iOS – it's now accessed solely via the dedicate Gemini app.
item.dc:creator: john-anthony.disotto@futurenet.com (John-Anthony Disotto), TechRadar

item.title: SpaceX Lands Rocket Booster in Bahamas
item.description: Historic feat: SpaceX launches rocket from Florida and lands booster in Bahamas
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bannon: Musk 'wants to impose his freak experiments' on US
item.description: Former White House chief adviser turned conservative commentator Steve Bannon blasted billionaire Elon Musk over his efforts to reduce the size of the federal government and gain more power in the U.S. "Musk is a parasitic illegal immigrant," he said during an interview with the website UnHerd.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Q&A: ManpowerGroup exec explains how to manage an AI workforce
item.description: Generative AI (genAI) projects will move from pilot phase to production for many companies this year, which means the workforce will be affected in ways never before imagined. One of those ways will involve onboarding AI agents as new digital employees.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Serverless was never a cure-all
item.description: An obituary for serverless computing might read something like this: In loving memory of serverless computing, born from the fervent hopes of developers and cloud architects alike.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk shared Mars plan over drinks, Star Trek convo: Entrepreneur
item.description: "I realized he was dead serious. Elon Musk doesn't just dream, he makes things happen," said entrepreneur and academic Vivek Wadhwa.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Trump's Stargate ambitions
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI coding assistants limited but helpful, developers say
item.description: AI coding assistants still need to mature, but they are already helpful according to several attendees of a recent Silicon Valley event for software developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban. TikTok, which is operated by Chinese technology firm ByteDance, was removed from Apple and Google’s app stores on Jan.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China launches center to train 100-plus humanoid robots simultaneously
item.description: A humanoid robot training center has opened in Shanghai, a big step in China's efforts to become a global leader in robotics and artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buckle up for faster Python programs
item.description: In this edition of our biweekly Python newsletter: The next version of Python brings an all-new variety of speed boost. Python packages may soon pack better labeling for their ingredients. Cython 3.1 tees up new features for converting Python to C.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Avoiding the cloud migration graveyard
item.description: Fictional global retailer StyleHaven embarked on its cloud migration journey, eager to modernize its systems and improve its customers’ experience. StyleHaven opted to “lift and shift,” assuming its existing infrastructure would transfer to the cloud with few issues. The result? Chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cisco financials catch AI demand, enterprise networking growth
item.description: Cisco executives were upbeat about the growth in AI orders — $700 million so far this year, on way to surpass $1 billion in 2025 – as the networking giant detailed a successful 2Q earnings period this week to Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Screenshot-scanning malware discovered on Apple App Store in first-of-its-kind attack
item.description: Security experts have discovered that hackers are targeting apps on the App Store to spread malware that steals information from saved screenshots.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Go 1.24 arrives with generic type aliases, boosted WebAssembly support
item.description: Go 1.24, an update to Google‘s popular open source programming language, is now generally available as a production release, with full backing for generic type aliases, performance improvements, and improved WebAssembly support. The release was unveiled February 11, and can be downloaded from Go.
item.dc:creator: Info World
